data.summary: Implemented deterministic topoSort with cycle detection and required tests.
Implemented and tested numeric core and prerelease semver precedence.
Implemented and tested a capacity-bounded LRU cache with get/set recency refresh.
Implemented and tested RFC-4180 CSV line parsing for quoted, escaped, comma-containing, and empty fields.
Implemented and tested case-insensitive multi-unit duration parsing to milliseconds.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/topoSort.test.ts | 22 ++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/topoSort.ts      | 30 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 52 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/topoSort.ts exports topoSort returning a valid deterministic (lexical tie-break) ordering of all nodes; throws with "cycle" in the message on a cyclic graph: topoSort is exported, implements Kahn's algorithm with the ready list re-sorted lexically after every insertion (deterministic lexical tie-break), covers all nodes derived from edges, and throws new Error("graph contains a cycle") — message contains "cycle" — when result.length !== nodes.size, which occurs exactly when a cycle prevents completion.
✓ calibration/topoSort.test.ts covers a linear chain, a diamond (a first / d last / predecessors before d), and a cycle throwing: Test file contains three tests: linear chain [a,b],[b,c] asserting [a,b,c]; diamond asserting a first, d last, b and c before d (plus exact ordering [a,b,c,d]); and a two-node cycle asserting toThrow(/cycle/i).

1 commit(s):
calibration/semverCompare.test.ts | 25 +++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/semverCompare.ts      | 35 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 60 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/semverCompare.ts exports semverCompare returning -1|0|1 with correct numeric core + prerelease precedence (prerelease < release, numeric<alphanumeric, longer-outranks-shorter): Exported function returns only -1|0|1. Numeric core compared via BigInt per component; prerelease presence check makes prerelease sort before release; identifier loop compares numeric identifiers numerically (BigInt), ranks numeric below alphanumeric via the lNumeric!==rNumeric branch, and the undefined-identifier branch makes a longer prerelease outrank a shorter matching one — all consistent with SemVer §11 precedence for the required behaviors.
✓ calibration/semverCompare.test.ts covers core ordering, equality, prerelease-vs-release, and two prerelease-identifier cases: Tests include core ordering (1.0.0 vs 1.0.1, 1.2.0 vs 1.1.9), equality (1.0.0 vs 1.0.0), prerelease-vs-release (1.0.0-alpha vs 1.0.0), and two prerelease-identifier cases: numeric-vs-alphanumeric (alpha.1 vs alpha.beta) and longer-vs-shorter (alpha.1 vs alpha) — and the implementation returns the asserted values for each.

data.summary: Implemented and tested stack-based RPN evaluation for integers, decimals, and four arithmetic operators.

data.details:
1 commit(s):
calibration/lruCache.test.ts | 40 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/lruCache.ts      | 26 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 66 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/lruCache.ts exports lruCache with get/set/size, capacity-bounded, recency refreshed by BOTH get and set, in-place update doesn't grow size: lruCache is exported with get/set/size. get does delete+re-insert to refresh recency; set also does delete+re-insert, so both refresh recency. set deletes the key before inserting, so an in-place update never grows the map, and any insert that pushes size past capacity immediately evicts the oldest key (Map insertion order = LRU order), keeping the cache capacity-bounded after every operation.
✓ calibration/lruCache.test.ts covers plain eviction, get-refreshes-recency eviction, and the size bound: Test 1 fills capacity 2, adds a third key, and asserts 'a' was evicted (plain eviction). Test 2 gets 'a' before inserting 'c' and asserts 'b' (not 'a') was evicted, proving get refreshes recency. Test 3 asserts size()===1 after an in-place update of 'a' and size()===2 after overflowing capacity, covering the size bound.

2 commit(s):
calibration/parseCsvLine.test.ts | 24 ++++++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/parseCsvLine.ts      | 27 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 51 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/parseCsvLine.ts exports parseCsvLine handling quoted fields, escaped "" quotes, embedded commas inside quotes, empty fields, and empty input. calibration/parseCsvLine.test.ts covers plain, embedded-comma, escaped-quote, empty-middle-field, and empty-input cases.: parseCsvLine.ts exports the function with a quote-state parser: entering/exiting quotes toggles `quoted`, a doubled "" inside quotes appends a literal quote and skips a char, commas split only when unquoted, trailing fields.push preserves empty fields, and empty input yields [""]. The test file contains exactly the five required cases (plain a,b,c; "a,b",c embedded comma; "she said ""hi""" escaped quote; a,,c empty middle; "" → [""]), and each expected value matches what the implementation produces when traced.

1 commit(s):
calibration/parseDuration.test.ts | 19 +++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/parseDuration.ts      | 20 ++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 39 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/parseDuration.ts exports parseDuration returning ms: File exports parseDuration(input: string): number summing amount × MS_PER_UNIT in milliseconds.
✓ handles ms/s/m/h/d units: MS_PER_UNIT covers ms/s/m/h/d; regex alternation (ms|[smhd]) lists ms first so '500ms' cannot mis-parse as m + leftover s.
✓ handles multi-unit input: Global regex replace accumulates every amount/unit pair ('1h30m' = 3_600_000 + 1_800_000 = 5_400_000, matches test expectation).
✓ case-insensitive: /gi flag plus unit.toLowerCase() before the table lookup; '1H30M' test asserts 5_400_000.
✓ handles whitespace: \s* between amount and unit, and inter-token whitespace ends up in remainder which is .trim()ed before the reject check; ' 2d 4h ' test asserts 187_200_000 (correct sum).
✓ throws on empty: Leading guard `if (!input.trim()) throw` covers '' and whitespace-only.
✓ throws on unknown unit: '5x' matches no pair, remainder '5x' is non-empty after trim → throws Invalid duration.
✓ throws on negative: '-1h' leaves the '-' in remainder ((\d+) cannot match a sign) → non-empty remainder throws.
✓ test file covers 90s, 1h30m, 500ms, 2d, case-insensitivity, and two throwing cases: test.each asserts 90s→90_000, 1h30m→5_400_000, 500ms→500, 2d→172_800_000, 1H30M→5_400_000 (case), plus three throwing inputs ('', '5x', '-1h') — exceeds the required two; all expected values are arithmetically correct.

2 commit(s):
calibration/rpnEval.test.ts | 21 +++++++++++++++++++++
 calibration/rpnEval.ts      | 26 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++
 2 files changed, 47 insertions(+)

✓ calibration/rpnEval.ts exports rpnEval evaluating +,-,*,/ via a stack over ints/decimals; throws on unknown token, operand underflow, and leftover operands. calibration/rpnEval.test.ts covers four valid expressions and two throwing (underflow + leftover) cases.: rpnEval.ts exports rpnEval using a number[] stack handling all four operators; NUMBER regex accepts ints and decimals (including .5 / 3. forms and signed numbers); throws Error on unknown token (else branch), underflow (stack.length < 2), and leftover operands (stack.length !== 1). Test file covers exactly four valid expressions via it.each ('3 4 +'=7, '3 4 + 5 *'=35, '10 2 /'=5, '2 3 4 * +'=14) plus an underflow case ('3 +') and a leftover case ('1 2 3 +'), both asserting toThrow.
